title: Market Brief — Friday, April 17, 2026
summary: Compute remains scarce across legacy silicon: H100 availability sits at a severe 12% (106/333 GPUs), H200 at a severe 15% (59/334), while B200 floods in at 33% oversupply-adjacent (186/471) as utilization cratered 17.7pp in 30 days. The headline move is H100 spot collapsing 13.2% day-over-day to $1.74/hr, signaling demand rotation toward Blackwell despite B200 pricing volatility running at 96% annualized.
paragraphs: Compute remains scarce across legacy silicon: H100 availability sits at a severe 12% (106/333 GPUs), H200 at a severe 15% (59/334), while B200 floods in at 33% oversupply-adjacent (186/471) as utilization cratered 17.7pp in 30 days. The headline move is H100 spot collapsing 13.2% day-over-day to $1.74/hr, signaling demand rotation toward Blackwell despite B200 pricing volatility running at 96% annualized.

At TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot), H100 Llama 70B inference runs $6.34/M and H200 hits $7.98/M, meaning medium-tier API sellers on both chips are burning cash, losing roughly $9.34/M on H200 at the $3/M midpoint. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.38/M output is selling at a loss against any GPU except B200 at TCO-adjusted $3.53/M. Provider grades: OpenAI Strong, Anthropic Neutral, Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash.

B200 at $3.86/hr spot with 33% availability and collapsing utilization is overvalued short-term; wait for the supply glut to push pricing toward $3.00 floor. H100 at $1.74/hr during a severe shortage with 88% utilization is undervalued; accumulate H100 spot capacity now before the squeeze tightens.
bullets: 12% of H100 GPUs are available, qualifying as a severe shortage at 106 free units out of 333.
33% of B200 GPUs sit idle as utilization dropped 17.7 percentage points in 30 days.
$1.74/hr H100 spot fell 13.2% in one day despite only 12% availability.
96% annualized volatility on B200 OCPI makes it the riskiest compute contract in the stack.
$0.38/M output from DeepSeek V3.2 undercuts the next cheapest model by 24%.
21.41 on the LLMflation Index means frontier token pricing has fallen 79% since GPT-4 launch.
